mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in <40% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by >0.5% over 3 months, or Rank IC <0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 4.8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that pressure corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in <30% of triggered instances, or UNRATE increases correlate positively with SPX returns (p>0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and payroll employment indicate weakening supply-side capacity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows no significant association with SPX returns (p>0.2), or 60d returns exceed -5% without triggering signals.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in housing starts above 2,000k units signals strong demand for construction, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in <35% of triggered instances, or HOUST increases correlate negatively with SPX returns (p>0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the money supply (RSAFS) indicates tightening financial conditions that reduce equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in <25% of instances where RSAFS declines by >2% over 3 months, or Rank IC <0.01 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production below 110.0 signals manufacturing weakness that depresses corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in <30% of triggered instances, or INDPRO declines correlate positively with SPX returns (p>0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 3.0% indicates strong economic growth that drives corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in <45% of instances where GDPC1 increases by >2.5% over 3 months, or Rank IC <0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market demand, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in <35% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS increases correlate negatively with SPX returns (p>0.1).
